behaviors.description: Speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so downstream summary attribution can map statements to the correct attendee.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is in model-produced diarization/labeling quality, not just local parsing. REAL-INFRA: it uses a real external API, but the critical risk is model output correctness. MOCK-HIDE: a stub transcript with perfect labels would always pass and hide real diarization failures. LLM-DEP: yes (speech model output varies by audio characteristics and may be non-deterministic). MULTI-STEP: no explicit multi-agentic chain is required to validate this behavior alone. Rejected higher level (Workflow): behavior text is limited to "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels" and does not require validating multiple model decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real multi-speaker meeting clip through the production STT API path and assert speaker labels are retained and correctly attached to utterance spans used for summary attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is parser/transform logic on transcript text and output structure, which can fail within one function boundary. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: mocking is not required; direct fixtures can expose parsing bugs. LLM-DEP: none if parser input is plain transcript text. MULTI-STEP: no. Rejected higher level (Integration): behavior text focuses on "Transcript parsing extracts..." and does not require cross-module runtime effects beyond local transform logic.
behaviors.test_description: Test the parsing function with transcript fixtures containing explicit assignments, including edge phrasing, and assert exact assignee/task fields in the produced summary-input object.

behaviors.description: Live summary output includes at least one action item for each attendee assigned a task.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the core failure is model omission/coverage in generated output. REAL-INFRA: real API call is involved, but output quality is the boundary. MOCK-HIDE: deterministic mocked summaries would hide missing-attendee action-item failures. LLM-DEP: yes, correctness depends on model generation decisions. MULTI-STEP: can be validated with one summarization call over a prepared transcript, so Workflow is not strictly required. Rejected higher level (Workflow): behavior text targets final summary coverage, not a required multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a transcript with known task assignments for multiple attendees to the live summarizer (real LLM) and assert the returned action_items contain at least one item per assigned attendee.

behaviors.description: Decision routing logic places identified decisions into the `decisions` schema section instead of generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure appears at module handoff (decision extractor output -> summary schema assembler), not pure single-function logic in many implementations. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; in-process module wiring is sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: mocking external services does not hide this; wiring/state propagation does. LLM-DEP: not required when using pre-identified decisions as input fixtures. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agentic chain required. Rejected higher levels: System is disqualified because behavior text is about routing logic/schema placement, not infrastructure behavior; Agentic is disqualified by "identified decisions" wording, which allows deterministic fixture input.
behaviors.test_description: Wire extractor-output objects into the real summary assembly modules in-process and assert decisions are serialized only under `decisions` and absent from generic narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions in the transcript appear in the live summary `decisions` section, not buried in narrative text.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: true failure is whether the model recognizes and places decisions correctly in generated structure. REAL-INFRA: external call exists, but model judgment is the risk boundary. MOCK-HIDE: fixed mocked output would mask omission/misplacement of real decisions. LLM-DEP: yes, correctness depends on model extraction/generation quality. MULTI-STEP: can be tested with a single real summarization step from transcript text. Rejected higher level (Workflow): behavior text specifies transcript-to-live-summary placement, not required sequencing of multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run real LLM summarization on transcripts with explicit decision statements and assert those decisions are present in the `decisions` field and not only in narrative sections.

behaviors.description: For audio longer than 60 minutes, service segments audio, processes sequentially, and merges into one coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failures often emerge across stage boundaries (segmenter -> per-segment transcription/summarization -> merger) and sequence/state carryover. REAL-INFRA: long-file handling and real API calls matter, but the defining risk is chained model-dependent processing. MOCK-HIDE: per-segment stubbed perfect outputs hide coherence, duplication, and boundary-loss failures in merged summary. LLM-DEP: yes, each segment’s model outputs affect final correctness. MULTI-STEP: explicitly yes (multiple agentic steps in sequence). Rejected lower levels: Agentic alone is insufficient because behavior text requires segmentation + sequential processing + merge across multiple steps.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording, run production segmentation and sequential processing, then verify merged output is a single valid structured summary with no cross-segment decision/action loss or duplication.

behaviors.description: Full pipeline (upload, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) runs end-to-end on a real meeting recording without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is explicitly end-to-end orchestration across multiple stages and interfaces. REAL-INFRA: requires real upload path, external APIs, and delivery boundary. MOCK-HIDE: mocks can pass while real pipeline fails on retries, handoffs, timing, or output compatibility. LLM-DEP: yes, summarization content/structure depends on real model output. MULTI-STEP: explicitly yes (multiple sequential stages, including agentic steps). Rejected lower levels: behavior text explicitly says "full pipeline" and "real meeting recording," disqualifying Unit/Integration and single-step Agentic tests.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the production end-to-end flow on a real recording: upload audio, run STT, run LLM summarization, and verify delivered structured payload is complete and schema-valid without human fixes.
